Two quick ways to check collinearity:

1.  Compare slopes  
   Slope between any two of the points must equal the slope between any other two.

   m₁₂ = (-3 − (-2)) / (5 − 3) = (-1) / 2 = –½  
   m₁₃ = (-1 − (-2)) / (1 − 3) = 1 / (-2) = –½

2.  Use the “triangle‐area” test  
   The (twice) area formed by the three points is  

      Δ = x₁(y₂ − y₃) + x₂(y₃ − y₁) + x₃(y₁ − y₂)

      = 3[-3 − (-1)] + 5[-1 − (-2)] + 1[-2 − (-3)]  
      = 3(-2) + 5(1) + 1(1)  
      = –6 + 5 + 1  
      = 0.

   Zero area means the points are on the same line.
